# Nodal — Full Documentation

Nodal is an operational orchestration platform by Enigmatic. It connects people, systems, and processes into automated flows that run your business.

Website: https://enigmatic.works
Docs: https://enigmatic.works/en/docs
API (JSON): https://enigmatic.works/api/docs/en

---

## Overview

Nodal is an operational orchestration platform. It connects your people, systems, and processes into automated flows that run your business.

### What is Flow Studio?

Flow Studio is the visual designer where you build business process flows. Each flow is a sequence of steps — triggers that start the process, tasks assigned to people, data transformations, API calls to external systems, and decision logic that routes work based on conditions.

### What are Action Flows?

Action Flows are running instances of a flow. When a flow is triggered — by an API call, a schedule, or a manual start — it creates an Action Flow that tracks the execution through each step. Team members see their assigned tasks in the Action Flows dashboard and complete them to advance the process.

### How a flow runs

1. Trigger fires — A flow starts when its trigger activates — an API call, a schedule, or a manual start.
2. Steps execute in sequence — Each node runs in order. Data flows from one step to the next. Conditions and switches route the path.
3. Pauses when needed — Human Tasks wait for a person to respond. Wait for Event nodes wait for an external webhook. The flow resumes automatically when the input arrives.
4. Completes or fails — The flow reaches the end and completes, or an error occurs and it fails. All execution data is logged.

---

## Core Concepts

Key ideas to understand before building flows.

- Nodes: The building blocks of a flow. Each node performs one action — triggering, transforming data, calling an API, assigning a task, or making a decision.
- Edges: Connections between nodes that define the execution order. Data flows along edges from one node to the next.
- Expressions: Dynamic references to data from previous steps. Written as {{ steps.NodeId.output.field }} to pass data between nodes.
- Action Flows: A running instance of a flow. Each trigger creates a new Action Flow that progresses through the steps until completion.
- Human-in-the-Loop: Flows can pause and wait for human input. Human Task nodes assign work to team members who complete it through the Action Flows dashboard.
- Webhooks: External systems can resume paused flows by sending data to a unique webhook URL. No API keys or IDs required.

---

## AI Agents (MCP)

Connect AI agents to Nodal documentation. Built for the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and LLM discovery.

### Discovery (llms.txt)

Nodal implements the /llms.txt standard. This file acts as a manifest, guiding AI agents to the most relevant documentation without needing to crawl HTML.

Discovery URL: https://enigmatic.works/llms.txt

### Static JSON API

Documentation is available as raw structured JSON. These endpoints are statically generated and cached on the edge for zero-latency access.

- English: https://enigmatic.works/api/docs/en
- Vietnamese: https://enigmatic.works/api/docs/vi
- Traditional Chinese: https://enigmatic.works/api/docs/zh-TW

---

## API Trigger

Start a flow via HTTP POST. Use this to integrate external systems, build automation pipelines, or create event-driven workflows.

### Endpoint

POST https://enigmatic.works/api/flows/{flowId}/execute
Authorization: Bearer <your-jwt-token>
Content-Type: application/json

The flowId is the unique identifier of the published flow. You can find it in the Flow Studio URL or the flow settings.

### Request body

Send any JSON payload. The fields you send become available to all downstream steps via expressions.

Example:
{
  "order_id": "ORD-12345",
  "customer_name": "Acme Corp",
  "total": 2499.00,
  "priority": "high"
}

### Configuration options

- schema (SchemaField[]): Define expected payload fields with types and required flags.
- instanceNameTemplate (string): Dynamic title for the Action Flow instance. Supports expressions.
- defaultPriority (enum): Priority level: low, medium, high, or critical.
- instanceDescriptionTemplate (string): Instructions displayed to users in the Action Flow dashboard.

### Accessing trigger data

{{ steps.trigger.data.order_id }}     // "ORD-12345"
{{ steps.trigger.data.customer_name }} // "Acme Corp"
{{ steps.trigger.data.total }}         // 2499.00

### Example: cURL

curl -X POST https://enigmatic.works/api/flows/YOUR_FLOW_ID/execute \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_JWT_TOKEN" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "order_id": "ORD-12345",
    "customer_name": "Acme Corp"
  }'

---

## Human Task

Pause the flow and assign work to a person. The flow resumes when the assignee completes the task through the Action Flows dashboard.

### How it works

1. Flow reaches the Human Task node — The flow pauses and creates a task record. The task appears in the assignee's Action Flows dashboard.
2. Assignee reviews and responds — The assignee sees the task title, instructions, and a form to fill out. They can review context from previous steps.
3. Flow resumes with the response — When the assignee submits their response, the flow continues. The form data is available to downstream steps.

### Configuration

- title (string): Task title shown to the assignee. Supports expressions for dynamic titles.
- instructions (rich text): Detailed instructions in HTML. Explain what the assignee needs to do.
- assignments (User[]): One or more team members who will see and can complete the task.
- schema (FormField[]): Form fields the assignee must fill out (text, number, date, rating, signature, etc.).

### Form field types

Supported: Text, Long Text, Number, Rating, Boolean, Date, Time, DateTime, File, Multiple Choice, Checkboxes, Signature.

### Dynamic titles with expressions

Use expressions to create context-aware task titles:

Review order {{ steps.trigger.data.order_id }} for {{ steps.trigger.data.customer_name }}

This produces titles like "Review order ORD-12345 for Acme Corp".

### Accessing task responses

{{ steps.ReviewTask.output.approval }}    // "approved"
{{ steps.ReviewTask.output.comments }}    // "Looks good"
{{ steps.ReviewTask.output.rating }}      // 5

---

## Wait for Event

Pause the flow and wait for an external system to send data via a unique webhook URL. No authentication, IDs, or complex payloads required.

### How it works

1. Flow reaches the Wait for Event node — The flow pauses and generates a unique, single-use webhook URL.
2. Webhook URL is sent to the external system — Use an upstream HTTP Request or Email node to pass the webhook URL to the external system.
3. External system POSTs data — The external system sends a POST request with JSON data to the webhook URL.
4. Flow resumes — The flow continues with the received data available to all downstream steps.

### Endpoint

POST https://enigmatic.works/api/webhooks/{token}

The {token} is a unique UUID generated for each flow execution. No authentication headers are required — the token itself authenticates the request. Each URL is single-use.

### Request format

Send any JSON body. The entire payload becomes available to downstream steps.

Example:
{
  "payment_status": "confirmed",
  "amount": 149.99,
  "transaction_id": "txn_abc123"
}

### Response

200 OK:
{
  "status": "ok",
  "message": "Workflow resumed successfully"
}

### Accessing the webhook URL in your flow

{{ steps.WaitForPayment.output.webhook_url }}

---

## HTTP Request

Make HTTP requests to external APIs. Call third-party services, fetch data, or send notifications as part of your flow.

### Configuration

- method (enum): HTTP method: GET, POST, PUT, DELETE, or PATCH.
- url (string): The endpoint URL. Supports expressions for dynamic URLs.
- headers (KeyValue[]): Custom HTTP headers (e.g., Authorization, API keys).
- params (KeyValue[]): URL query parameters.
- body (JSON): Request body for POST/PUT/PATCH. Supports expressions.

### Output

{{ steps.CallAPI.output.status }}       // 200
{{ steps.CallAPI.output.data }}         // Parsed JSON response body
{{ steps.CallAPI.output.data.result }}  // Nested field access

---

## Correlation Signals

For advanced use cases where multiple flow instances listen for the same event type. Use correlation matching to target the correct instance.

### When to use

- Multiple flow instances waiting for the same event type (e.g., 'OrderPaid')
- The external system broadcasts events without knowing which flow to target
- You need to match on business keys (e.g., order_id, customer_id)

### Endpoint

POST https://enigmatic.works/api/automation/signal
Authorization: Bearer <your-jwt-token>
Content-Type: application/json

### Request format

{
  "event": "OrderPaid",
  "data": {
    "order_id": "ORD-12345",
    "customer_id": "CUST-789"
  }
}

- event (string): The event name configured in the Wait for Event node. Defaults to "default".
- data (object): Key-value pairs matched against the flow's correlation criteria. All criteria must match (AND logic).

### Matching logic

1. Find active subscriptions — The system finds all active subscriptions matching the event name.
2. Check criteria — For each subscription, every configured criteria key must exist in the signal data with the same value.
3. Resume matches — All matching flows are resumed. One signal can resume multiple flow instances.

### Response

200 OK:
{
  "status": "ok",
  "resumed": 1
}

---

## Set Variable

Create or update variables within the flow. Store computed values, rename fields, or prepare data for downstream steps.

### Single variable

Variable Name:  total_with_tax
Value:          {{ steps.trigger.data.total * 1.13 }}

### Multiple variables

customer_name  →  {{ steps.trigger.data.name }}
order_total    →  {{ steps.trigger.data.total }}
status         →  "pending_review"

### Output

{{ steps.SetVars.output.total_with_tax }}  // 2823.87
{{ steps.SetVars.output.customer_name }}   // "Acme Corp"

---

## Condition (If/Else)

Branch the flow based on a logical condition. Route work down the True path or the False path.

### Configuration

The Condition node uses a visual builder to define logic. It compares two values using an operator.

- Value A (expression): The left side of the comparison.
- Operator (select): The logic to apply (e.g., Is Equal To, Is Greater Than).
- Value B (expression): The right side to compare against.

### Available Operators

- == (equals): Check if values are identical.
- != (not equals): Check if values are different.
- > (greater than): True if Value A is strictly greater than Value B.
- < (less than): True if Value A is strictly less than Value B.
- contains (includes): True if Value A (string/array) contains Value B.
- matches (regex): True if Value A matches the Regular Expression in Value B.

### Branching

The flow splits into two paths: True and False. You can connect different actions to each path.

---

## Switch

Route the flow to different paths based on a variable's value. Like a multi-way if/else — cleaner when you have more than two branches.

### Configuration

Variable:  {{ steps.trigger.data.department }}

Case "engineering"  →  Engineering Review path
Case "finance"      →  Finance Approval path
Case "legal"        →  Legal Review path
Default             →  General Processing path

### Behavior

1. Variable comparison — The variable is compared against each case value in order.
2. First match — The first matching case path is followed.
3. Default path — If no cases match, the Default path is followed.
4. Output edge — Each case creates a separate output edge in the flow designer.

---

## Loop

Iterate over an array of items and run a set of steps for each one. Process lists of orders, users, records, or any collection.

### Configuration

Items:  {{ steps.FetchOrders.output.data }}

### Inside the loop

{{ steps.MyLoop.item }}           // The current item object
{{ steps.MyLoop.item.order_id }}  // A field on the current item
{{ steps.MyLoop.item.total }}     // Another field

---

## Filter

Filter an array based on conditions, creating a new array with only matching items.

### Configuration

- Array Variable (expression): The list to filter (e.g. {{ steps.GetUsers.data }}).
- Match Type (enum): ALL (AND) requires every condition to be true. ANY (OR) requires at least one.

### Condition Builder

Add one or more conditions to define which items to keep:

1. Item Field — The property on the item to check (e.g. 'status' or 'price').
2. Operator — The logic (Equals, Contains, Greater Than, etc.).
3. Value — The value to compare against.

---

## Map (Transform)

Transform each item in an array to a new structure.

### Configuration

1. Input Array (Optional) — If provided, the Map operation runs for every item in this list. If left empty, it runs once for a single object.
2. Mapping Fields — Add fields to define the output object. The 'Key' is your new property name, and the 'Value' is the data source.

### Example

Key (Target): full_name
Value (Source): {{ item.first_name }} {{ item.last_name }}

---

## Expression Syntax

Expressions are wrapped in double curly braces {{ }}. They support JavaScript-like syntax for accessing object properties and array elements.

{{ steps.trigger.data.id }}
{{ steps.MyStep.output.result }}
{{ steps.MyStep.output.items[0] }}
